title: Grafana API Token and Service Account Token Lifecycle Monitoring
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.6.5 login audit narratives, UC-13.6.11 directory team synchronization, UC-13.6.10 permission drift analytics, UC-13.6.13 notification failure tracing, UC-13.6.1 or UC-13.6.2 datasource health and query error monitoring, UC-13.6.3 alert evaluation drift, UC-13.6.4 plugin governance, or UC-13.6.6 annotation storms. Does not automatically revoke Grafana tokens; it produces evidence and routing guidance for owner-approved rotation.
evidence: Saved search observability_uc_13_6_8_grafana_token_lifecycle with fifteen or thirty minute schedule; Grafana token lifecycle dashboard drilldowns; weekly CSV and JSON exports archived to a restricted evidence index.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab Grafana org, create a service account token, ingest matching grafana:audit, grafana:log, and grafana:token_snapshot rows with the same token_id_hash while grafana_token_inventory.csv marks admin_flag=yes for an offboard test alias; confirm observability_uc_13_6_8_grafana_token_lifecycle returns critical or high severity within thirty minutes.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After revoking the lab token and setting legacy_ok=yes only on approved rows, confirm severities remain low for the revoked token_key across six consecutive scheduled runs with only routine snapshot noise.

dataSources: `index=observability` `sourcetype=grafana:audit` for service account and API token create, revoke, rotation, and expires_at style fields; `sourcetype=grafana:log` auth middleware with token_id_hash, client IP, path, and optional requests_per_min; `sourcetype=grafana:token_snapshot` nightly exports from `/api/auth/keys` and `/api/serviceaccounts/{id}/tokens`; optional `sourcetype=grafana:token_lineage` HTTP Event Collector events from CI or exporters declaring consumer_system and repository; lookup `grafana_token_inventory.csv`; optional accelerated CIM `Authentication` and `Change` summaries for Grafana hosts.
app: Grafana Enterprise audit logging, Splunk Universal Forwarder or HTTP Event Collector ingestion patterns aligned with Splunk Add-on for Grafana naming, plus a hardened snapshot collector script for token inventory exports.
spl:
`comment("UC-13.6.8 Grafana service account and API token lifecycle usage multisearch earliest=-24h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=observability sourcetype=grafana:audit earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="audit_token_lifecycle"
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval token_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(token_id_hash,token_hash,key_fingerprint,md5(toString(coalesce(apiKeyId,tokenId,token_id,name,"audit")))))))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,"0")))
      | eval actor_normalized=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(userId,user_id,username,actor,email,"unknown_actor"))))
      | eval action=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(action,verb,operation,"unknown"))))
      | eval token_class=if(match(lr,"service.account|serviceaccount"),"service_account_token",if(match(lr,"api.key|apikey|legacy.key"),"legacy_api_key","token_event"))
      | eval expires_at_raw=trim(toString(coalesce(expires_at,expiration,expiry,"")))
      | eval expires_ts=if(len(expires_at_raw)>10,strptime(replace(expires_at_raw,"\\.\\d+Z$","Z"),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),null())
      | eval ttl_days=if(isnotnull(expires_ts),round((expires_ts-now())/86400,2),999)
      | eval clientip=trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,src_ip,ip,"")))
      | eval rpm=0
      | eval integration_name=""
      | eval downstream_system=""
      | eval last_used_epoch=null() ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype=grafana:log earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="middleware_token_usage"
      | eval token_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(token_id_hash,token_fingerprint,jwt_jti_hash,""))))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id,orgId,"0")))
      | eval actor_normalized=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(remote_user,username,actor,"token_actor"))))
      | eval action="api_request"
      | eval token_class=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(auth_mechanism,token_type,"bearer_token"))))
      | eval expires_at_raw=""
      | eval expires_ts=null()
      | eval ttl_days=999
      | eval clientip=trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,src_ip,remote_addr,"0.0.0.0")))
      | eval rpm=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(requests_per_min,rpm_est,"0"))),10)
      | eval integration_name=""
      | eval downstream_system=""
      | eval last_used_epoch=if(isnotnull(_time),_time,null()) ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype=grafana:token_snapshot earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="api_token_snapshot"
      | eval token_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(token_id_hash,snapshot_token_hash,key_id_hash,""))))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,"0")))
      | eval actor_normalized="snapshot_job"
      | eval action="inventory_snapshot"
      | eval token_class=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(snapshot_token_class,token_kind,"service_account_token"))))
      | eval expires_at_raw=trim(toString(coalesce(expires_at,expiration,"")))
      | eval expires_ts=if(len(expires_at_raw)>10,strptime(replace(expires_at_raw,"\\.\\d+Z$","Z"),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),null())
      | eval ttl_days=if(isnotnull(expires_ts),round((expires_ts-now())/86400,2),tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(declared_ttl_days,"999"))),10))
      | eval clientip="127.0.0.1"
      | eval rpm=0
      | eval integration_name=trim(toString(coalesce(named_integration,pipeline_hint,"")))
      | eval downstream_system=""
      | eval last_used_epoch=null() ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype=grafana:token_lineage earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="downstream_ci_exporter"
      | eval token_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(token_id_hash,declared_token_fp,""))))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id,orgId,"0")))
      | eval actor_normalized=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(submitter_actor,build_user,"cicd"))))
      | eval action="downstream_declaration"
      | eval token_class=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(declared_token_class,"integration"))))
      | eval expires_at_raw=""
      | eval expires_ts=null()
      | eval ttl_days=999
      | eval clientip=trim(toString(coalesce(source_ip,runner_ip,"")))
      | eval rpm=0
      | eval integration_name=trim(toString(coalesce(git_repo,helm_release,"")))
      | eval downstream_system=trim(toString(coalesce(consumer_system,exporter_name,ci_orchestrator,"")))
      | eval last_used_epoch=null() ]
| eval token_key=if(len(token_key)<8,md5(coalesce(actor_normalized,lane,_time)),token_key)
| eval org_id=coalesce(nullif(trim(org_id),""),"0")
| eval ttl_days=coalesce(ttl_days,999)
| eval rpm=coalesce(rpm,0)
| sort 0 token_key _time
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f last(_time) AS prior_evt_time values(clientip) AS recent_ips dc(clientip) AS ip_diversity BY token_key lane
| eventstats median(rpm) AS rpm_med p90(rpm) AS rpm_p90 BY org_id lane
| eval days_since_use=if(isnotnull(prior_evt_time),round((now()-prior_evt_time)/86400,2),null())
| join type=left max=0 token_key
    [| inputlookup grafana_token_inventory.csv
     | eval token_key=lower(trim(toString(token_key)))
     | eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team,team,"")))
     | eval owner_login=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(owner_login,primary_owner,""))))
     | eval allowed_cidr=trim(toString(coalesce(allowed_cidr,expected_cidrs,"")))
     | eval ttl_policy_days=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(ttl_policy_days,"90"))),10)
     | eval integration_expected=trim(toString(coalesce(integration_name,expected_integration,"")))
     | fields token_key owner_team owner_login allowed_cidr ttl_policy_days integration_expected admin_flag legacy_ok ]
| join type=left max=0 owner_login
    [| tstats summariesonly=true count AS auth_evt_cnt FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE nodename=Authentication earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Authentication.user
     | rename Authentication.user AS owner_login
     | eval owner_login=lower(trim(toString(owner_login))) ]
| eval auth_evt_cnt=coalesce(auth_evt_cnt,0)
| eval admin_flag=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(admin_flag,"no"))))
| eval legacy_ok=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(legacy_ok,"no"))))
| eval ttl_policy_days=coalesce(ttl_policy_days,90)
| eval cidr_violation=if(len(clientip)>0 AND len(allowed_cidr)>0 AND !match(clientip,allowed_cidr) AND match(lane,"middleware"),1,0)
| eval unused_stale=if(days_since_use>90 AND match(lane,"middleware|api_token_snapshot"),1,0)
| eval short_ttl_ci=if(ttl_days<14 AND ttl_days>=0 AND match(integration_expected,"prod_ci|release|main"),1,0)
| eval legacy_plain_active=if(match(token_class,"legacy_api_key") AND legacy_ok="no",1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    admin_flag="yes" AND match(actor_normalized,"terminated|ex_employee|offboard"), "critical",
    legacy_plain_active==1 AND rpm_p90>0, "critical",
    cidr_violation==1 AND rpm_med>0, "high",
    short_ttl_ci==1, "high",
    unused_stale==1 AND ttl_days>365, "medium",
    ttl_days<7 AND ttl_days>=0, "high",
    ip_diversity>=4 AND match(lane,"middleware"), "medium",
    auth_evt_cnt>5000, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity="critical" AND legacy_plain_active==1, "revoke_legacy_api_key_migrate_service_account",
    severity="critical", "suspend_token_validate_hr_and_owner",
    cidr_violation==1, "review_ip_allowlist_rotate_token_secret",
    short_ttl_ci==1, "extend_or_rotate_token_meet_ci_ttl_policy",
    unused_stale==1, "revoke_or_archive_unused_token",
    true(), "refresh_inventory_snapshot_and_lineage_hec")
| table _time org_id lane token_key token_class action ttl_days days_since_use clientip ip_diversity rpm_med owner_team owner_login severity cidr_violation recommended_action auth_evt_cnt
description: Correlates Grafana Enterprise audit token create, revoke, and expiry narratives with hashed middleware usage, nightly snapshots from HTTP API inventory exports, and optional continuous integration lineage so operators see TTL debt, deprecated plain API keys still authenticating, admin-scope automation tied to departed owners, stale unused credentials, requests from unfamiliar networks, and which exporter or pipeline still presents each fingerprint.
value: Platform leaders shorten outages caused by surprise token expiry, meet rotation attestations without spreadsheet hunting, and give auditors a joined story that ties Grafana audit policy events to real request fingerprints and downstream owners instead of isolated log lines.
implementation: Land grafana:audit, grafana:log, grafana:token_snapshot, and optional grafana:token_lineage into index=observability; publish grafana_token_inventory.csv; accelerate Authentication summaries for Grafana owners; save observability_uc_13_6_8_grafana_token_lifecycle every fifteen to thirty minutes with earliest twenty-four hours; route critical and high severities to platform on-call; archive weekly evidence exports.
visualization: Gantt-style chart of token TTL versus policy horizon; sortable table of token_key by severity and ttl_days; heatmap of clientip usage frequency per token fingerprint; single-value tiles for legacy plain key counts.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Change.object) AS chg_object count AS chg_cnt FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change (Change.object="*grafana*" OR Change.app="*grafana*") earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Change.user Change.dest
| rename Change.user AS actor Change.dest AS grafana_host
| table actor grafana_host chg_object chg_cnt
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Authentication summaries for Grafana automation principals and Change summaries for Grafana configuration and token policy edits; retain at least thirty days for quarterly access attestations paired with inventory snapshots.
knownFalsePositives: Planned token rotation programs temporarily increase create and revoke churn until cutover completes; correlate with change tickets before paging application teams. New cloud egress IPs after NAT pool expansion mimic unexpected IP signals until you refresh allowed_cidr in the lookup. Grafana version upgrades can rename audit fields; extend field aliases rather than muting detections. Nightly snapshot delays during maintenance windows make ttl_days look artificially flat; compare against Grafana UI before treating stale numbers as policy violations. Development orgs intentionally keep long-lived lab tokens; use org_id or owner_team filters for production-focused alerting. Health-check traffic from synthetic probes can inflate rpm_med for tokens shared with dashboards; tag probe identities in inventory. Break-glass tokens used during incidents create short bursts of admin-class usage that resemble policy violations until you annotate the incident ticket identifier in lineage events. Dual-active Grafana clusters during failover drills can duplicate middleware events; include cluster_id in future ingest revisions to deduplicate fairly. Service principals that never authenticate through interactive flows may show zero Authentication model joins even when tokens are healthy; treat null auth_evt_cnt as informational. Penetration tests and red-team exercises that mint short-lived tokens can resemble legacy key abuse until you label the engagement window in inventory.

premiumApps.name: Splunk Enterprise Security
premiumApps.note: optional — correlate token anomalies with risk notables when identity indexes are available

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — episode links for token rotation maintenance windows

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control with Grafana administrators, identity engineers who approve service account naming standards, and automation owners who embed tokens in continuous integration, Prometheus federation jobs, or external dashboard exporters. This use case is deliberately not UC-13.6.1 or UC-13.6.2 datasource query error analytics, not UC-13.6.3 alert evaluation drift, not UC-13.6.4 plugin catalog skew, not UC-13.6.5 interactive login audit narratives, not UC-13.6.6 annotation storms, not UC-13.6.10 folder and dashboard permission drift, not UC-13.6.11 LDAP or OAuth team synchronization, and not UC-13.6.13 notification channel failures. It is the lifecycle and usage telemetry story for Grafana API tokens and service account tokens: when credentials are minted and revoked, how long they live, whether deprecated plain API keys still authenticate, which automation fingerprint appears on each request, and which downstream system declared ownership in inventory.

Grafana Enterprise audit streams record many token operations under stable verbs when audit logging is enabled at the deployment tier that your license supports. Service accounts centralize automation identities; tokens issued under them inherit role bindings you assign in the organization. Legacy org-level API keys remain in some estates even as newer releases steer operators toward service accounts; those legacy objects often lack fine-grained attribution and expire on different fields than modern tokens. You need a consistent token_key fingerprint in every lane, never the raw secret. Typical fingerprints include vendor-supplied identifiers, salted hashes produced at creation time, or a stable surrogate you compute when ingesting audit JSON.

Operational prerequisites include time synchronization across Grafana nodes, log forwarders, snapshot workers, and Splunk indexers so expiry math and last-used deltas agree. Network paths must allow a hardened collector host to call Grafana HTTPS endpoints for nightly snapshots of /api/auth/keys and /api/serviceaccounts/{id}/tokens using a read automation credential stored in a vault, not in shell history. Legal and privacy reviewers should approve retention for hashed token identifiers, client IP addresses, service account display names, and repository identifiers present in optional lineage events forwarded through HTTP Event Collector.

Inventory discipline requires lookups/grafana_token_inventory.csv keyed on token_key with owner_team, owner_login, allowed_cidr, ttl_policy_days, integration_expected, admin_flag, and legacy_ok columns. The admin_flag marks tokens whose Grafana role is effectively Admin or equivalent fixed roles. The legacy_ok column documents exceptions where a plain API key is grandfathered during migration with an explicit expiry. Without this file, Splunk can show usage spikes but cannot answer which team must rotate a credential during an employee departure.

Risk briefing: major Grafana upgrades can change token creation audit field names; tune field aliases rather than rewriting detections. Blue-green Grafana clusters can double-count usage during cutover unless you tag cluster_id in middleware logs. Break-glass automation accounts must still appear in audit and middleware lanes so their TTL is visible.

Differentiation recap: token lifecycle, hashed usage telemetry, nightly API inventory, downstream lineage declarations, and integration impact mapping rather than login ratios, team sync, permission ACL drift, alert evaluation health, datasource errors, plugin skew, annotation rates, or notification delivery traces.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up four complementary lanes that multisearch fans in parallel, one CSV knowledge object, optional HTTP Event Collector token lineage from continuous integration, and accelerated summaries on Authentication and Change models where Grafana mutations map cleanly.

Lane A, Grafana audit JSON for tokens: enable Grafana audit logging to file, stdout, or a supported streaming sink and land events in index=observability with sourcetype=grafana:audit. Preserve actions that reference apiKey, service account tokens, token creation, deletion, rotation, and expiry fields including expires_at style timestamps when present. Normalize org_id, actor identities, token identifiers, and verbs. When Enterprise records fine-grained access control changes adjacent to token issuance, retain enough context to distinguish Viewer automation from Editor or Admin automation.

Lane B, Grafana auth middleware log: terminate TLS at a controlled ingress or enable Grafana structured request logging that emits sourcetype=grafana:log with token_id_hash or an equivalent fingerprint, HTTP method, path, status, latency, client IP, and optional requests_per_min estimates from your sidecar. Never write bearer secrets to disk. This lane supplies last-used style telemetry and unexpected IP detection independent of audit verbosity.

Lane C, nightly token snapshot: schedule a hardened worker every twenty-four hours to call GET /api/auth/keys for legacy key inventory where still enabled and enumerate service accounts then GET /api/serviceaccounts/{id}/tokens for each automation identity you manage. Emit one sourcetype=grafana:token_snapshot event per token row with token_id_hash, org_id, token_kind, expires_at, last_used_at if returned, named_integration hints from your CMDB mapping, and declared_ttl_days when you compute relative expiry. Hash exports with a canonical JSON representation when compliance wants tamper hints.

Lane D, downstream lineage optional: from GitLab CI, GitHub Actions, Argo CD hooks, or configuration management, emit a compact sourcetype=grafana:token_lineage event over HTTP Event Collector declaring token_id_hash, consumer_system, git_repo or helm_release, submitter_actor, and source_ip of the runner. This answers which pipeline still presents a token after Grafana revokes the logical object.

Knowledge object: lookups/grafana_token_inventory.csv with columns token_key, owner_team, owner_login, allowed_cidr, ttl_policy_days, integration_expected, admin_flag, legacy_ok.

Model alignment: map Grafana authentication telemetry that lands in the Common Information Model Authentication data model when field extractions populate Authentication.user and Authentication.src. Map configuration edits for token policies into the Change data model when change records include Grafana hosts and service restart narratives. Accelerate summaries where Job Inspector shows expensive joins against high-volume middleware logs.

Example curl helpers from a bastion after authenticating with a scoped admin token:

```bash
curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer $GRAFANA_ADMIN_TOKEN" https://grafana.example.org/api/auth/keys
curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer $GRAFANA_ADMIN_TOKEN" https://grafana.example.org/api/serviceaccounts/search?query=observability
curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer $GRAFANA_ADMIN_TOKEN" https://grafana.example.org/api/serviceaccounts/42/tokens
```

Pre-save validation: confirm each sourcetype returns events in a one-hour window, that a deliberate test token creation appears in grafana:audit, that middleware logs show the same token_id_hash on the next API call, and that the nightly snapshot job lists the test token with matching fingerprint.

Dashboards: host an analyst view grouped by org_id, token_key, and severity with drilldowns to raw audit, middleware lines, snapshot rows, and lineage events.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_6_8_grafana_token_lifecycle with a fifteen or thirty minute schedule in production, dispatch.earliest_time=-24h@h, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is critical or high. Throttle duplicate token_key and lane pairs for forty-five minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action in pager narratives so responders open Grafana token runbooks without rerunning ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans audit token lifecycle, middleware usage, nightly snapshot inventory, and downstream lineage declarations so a silent single lane cannot hide rotation debt. coalesce absorbs field naming drift across OSS, Enterprise, and Cloud samples. streamstats and eventstats compute rolling last-used deltas and request-rate baselines per token_key and lane. inputlookup joins attach owner teams, allowlists, integration expectations, admin flags, and legacy migration posture. A join with tstats against the Authentication data model adds authentication event density per owner_login for context during investigations. case maps short TTL on production automation, legacy key usage, allowlist violations, stale unused tokens, and IP diversity spikes into severity tiers.

Fenced SPL must match the top-level spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.6.8 Grafana service account and API token lifecycle usage multisearch earliest=-24h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=observability sourcetype=grafana:audit earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="audit_token_lifecycle"
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval token_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(token_id_hash,token_hash,key_fingerprint,md5(toString(coalesce(apiKeyId,tokenId,token_id,name,"audit")))))))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,"0")))
      | eval actor_normalized=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(userId,user_id,username,actor,email,"unknown_actor"))))
      | eval action=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(action,verb,operation,"unknown"))))
      | eval token_class=if(match(lr,"service.account|serviceaccount"),"service_account_token",if(match(lr,"api.key|apikey|legacy.key"),"legacy_api_key","token_event"))
      | eval expires_at_raw=trim(toString(coalesce(expires_at,expiration,expiry,"")))
      | eval expires_ts=if(len(expires_at_raw)>10,strptime(replace(expires_at_raw,"\\.\\d+Z$","Z"),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),null())
      | eval ttl_days=if(isnotnull(expires_ts),round((expires_ts-now())/86400,2),999)
      | eval clientip=trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,src_ip,ip,"")))
      | eval rpm=0
      | eval integration_name=""
      | eval downstream_system=""
      | eval last_used_epoch=null() ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype=grafana:log earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="middleware_token_usage"
      | eval token_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(token_id_hash,token_fingerprint,jwt_jti_hash,""))))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id,orgId,"0")))
      | eval actor_normalized=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(remote_user,username,actor,"token_actor"))))
      | eval action="api_request"
      | eval token_class=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(auth_mechanism,token_type,"bearer_token"))))
      | eval expires_at_raw=""
      | eval expires_ts=null()
      | eval ttl_days=999
      | eval clientip=trim(toString(coalesce(client_ip,src_ip,remote_addr,"0.0.0.0")))
      | eval rpm=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(requests_per_min,rpm_est,"0"))),10)
      | eval integration_name=""
      | eval downstream_system=""
      | eval last_used_epoch=if(isnotnull(_time),_time,null()) ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype=grafana:token_snapshot earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="api_token_snapshot"
      | eval token_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(token_id_hash,snapshot_token_hash,key_id_hash,""))))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,"0")))
      | eval actor_normalized="snapshot_job"
      | eval action="inventory_snapshot"
      | eval token_class=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(snapshot_token_class,token_kind,"service_account_token"))))
      | eval expires_at_raw=trim(toString(coalesce(expires_at,expiration,"")))
      | eval expires_ts=if(len(expires_at_raw)>10,strptime(replace(expires_at_raw,"\\.\\d+Z$","Z"),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),null())
      | eval ttl_days=if(isnotnull(expires_ts),round((expires_ts-now())/86400,2),tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(declared_ttl_days,"999"))),10))
      | eval clientip="127.0.0.1"
      | eval rpm=0
      | eval integration_name=trim(toString(coalesce(named_integration,pipeline_hint,"")))
      | eval downstream_system=""
      | eval last_used_epoch=null() ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype=grafana:token_lineage earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="downstream_ci_exporter"
      | eval token_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(token_id_hash,declared_token_fp,""))))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id,orgId,"0")))
      | eval actor_normalized=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(submitter_actor,build_user,"cicd"))))
      | eval action="downstream_declaration"
      | eval token_class=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(declared_token_class,"integration"))))
      | eval expires_at_raw=""
      | eval expires_ts=null()
      | eval ttl_days=999
      | eval clientip=trim(toString(coalesce(source_ip,runner_ip,"")))
      | eval rpm=0
      | eval integration_name=trim(toString(coalesce(git_repo,helm_release,"")))
      | eval downstream_system=trim(toString(coalesce(consumer_system,exporter_name,ci_orchestrator,"")))
      | eval last_used_epoch=null() ]
| eval token_key=if(len(token_key)<8,md5(coalesce(actor_normalized,lane,_time)),token_key)
| eval org_id=coalesce(nullif(trim(org_id),""),"0")
| eval ttl_days=coalesce(ttl_days,999)
| eval rpm=coalesce(rpm,0)
| sort 0 token_key _time
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f last(_time) AS prior_evt_time values(clientip) AS recent_ips dc(clientip) AS ip_diversity BY token_key lane
| eventstats median(rpm) AS rpm_med p90(rpm) AS rpm_p90 BY org_id lane
| eval days_since_use=if(isnotnull(prior_evt_time),round((now()-prior_evt_time)/86400,2),null())
| join type=left max=0 token_key
    [| inputlookup grafana_token_inventory.csv
     | eval token_key=lower(trim(toString(token_key)))
     | eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team,team,"")))
     | eval owner_login=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(owner_login,primary_owner,""))))
     | eval allowed_cidr=trim(toString(coalesce(allowed_cidr,expected_cidrs,"")))
     | eval ttl_policy_days=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(ttl_policy_days,"90"))),10)
     | eval integration_expected=trim(toString(coalesce(integration_name,expected_integration,"")))
     | fields token_key owner_team owner_login allowed_cidr ttl_policy_days integration_expected admin_flag legacy_ok ]
| join type=left max=0 owner_login
    [| tstats summariesonly=true count AS auth_evt_cnt FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE nodename=Authentication earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Authentication.user
     | rename Authentication.user AS owner_login
     | eval owner_login=lower(trim(toString(owner_login))) ]
| eval auth_evt_cnt=coalesce(auth_evt_cnt,0)
| eval admin_flag=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(admin_flag,"no"))))
| eval legacy_ok=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(legacy_ok,"no"))))
| eval ttl_policy_days=coalesce(ttl_policy_days,90)
| eval cidr_violation=if(len(clientip)>0 AND len(allowed_cidr)>0 AND !match(clientip,allowed_cidr) AND match(lane,"middleware"),1,0)
| eval unused_stale=if(days_since_use>90 AND match(lane,"middleware|api_token_snapshot"),1,0)
| eval short_ttl_ci=if(ttl_days<14 AND ttl_days>=0 AND match(integration_expected,"prod_ci|release|main"),1,0)
| eval legacy_plain_active=if(match(token_class,"legacy_api_key") AND legacy_ok="no",1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    admin_flag="yes" AND match(actor_normalized,"terminated|ex_employee|offboard"), "critical",
    legacy_plain_active==1 AND rpm_p90>0, "critical",
    cidr_violation==1 AND rpm_med>0, "high",
    short_ttl_ci==1, "high",
    unused_stale==1 AND ttl_days>365, "medium",
    ttl_days<7 AND ttl_days>=0, "high",
    ip_diversity>=4 AND match(lane,"middleware"), "medium",
    auth_evt_cnt>5000, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity="critical" AND legacy_plain_active==1, "revoke_legacy_api_key_migrate_service_account",
    severity="critical", "suspend_token_validate_hr_and_owner",
    cidr_violation==1, "review_ip_allowlist_rotate_token_secret",
    short_ttl_ci==1, "extend_or_rotate_token_meet_ci_ttl_policy",
    unused_stale==1, "revoke_or_archive_unused_token",
    true(), "refresh_inventory_snapshot_and_lineage_hec")
| table _time org_id lane token_key token_class action ttl_days days_since_use clientip ip_diversity rpm_med owner_team owner_login severity cidr_violation recommended_action auth_evt_cnt```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_6_8_grafana_token_lifecycle_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-observability-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = Grafana token lifecycle $result.severity$ token=$result.token_key$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_6_8_grafana_token_lifecycle | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if multisearch queue time exceeds targets, materialize each lane hourly into a summary index keyed on token_key and org_id, widen detection windows on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in Grafana service account screens and legacy API key lists for the same token fingerprint and window. Pick a Splunk row with short_ttl_ci=1 and confirm the live token expiry in Grafana matches snapshot ttl_days within one day, accounting for time zones.

Middleware validation: select a middleware_token_usage row, open Grafana audit for the same minute range, and confirm the token_id_hash aligns with the automation user you expect. Mismatches often indicate hashing salt rotation rather than Grafana defects.

Snapshot validation: export /api/serviceaccounts/{id}/tokens for a lab service account and compare JSON to the latest grafana:token_snapshot event.

Synthetic positive test: in lab, create a legacy-style API key if your version still permits it, mark legacy_ok=no in the lookup for that fingerprint, generate middleware traffic, and confirm critical or high severity within two windows.

Synthetic negative test: revoke the lab key, refresh the lookup, and confirm severities remain low for that token_key across six consecutive runs.

Runbook linkage: document expected noise during planned token rotation programs so validators compare against change calendars.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case A — Production continuous integration token TTL under fourteen days while pipeline runs nightly: extend service account token lifetime to meet policy, or rotate using a vault secret that refreshes automatically, and document the exception in grafana_token_inventory.csv until automation catches up.

Case B — Admin-role token still active for an ex-employee automation alias: disable the service account in Grafana, purge cached secrets in the runner namespace, and reconcile human resources offboarding tickets with token inventory owners.

Case C — Unused token older than ninety days still minted in snapshot exports: revoke the token, remove exporter configuration references, and close the configuration item once middleware confirms zero requests for seven days.

Case D — Token used from an unexpected IP outside allowed_cidr: verify whether the address is a new cloud NAT gateway, tighten allowlists if legitimate, or rotate the token if the address is unknown and lineage does not explain it.

Case E — Token secret exported to a git repository in plaintext: scrub history using your organization-approved secret removal procedure, rotate the token immediately, and wire the pipeline to a vault reference instead of a static file.

Case F — Plain legacy API key still authenticating after Grafana eleven migration planning flagged deprecation: migrate callers to a service account token, set legacy_ok=yes only with a written expiry, and block new legacy creations via configuration.

Case G — Dual-token cutover left both old and new fingerprints active: complete the deprecation checklist, revoke the superseded fingerprint, and confirm downstream lineage events reference only the new token_key.

Case H — Service account disabled in Grafana but upstream Prometheus or Agent still caches the bearer string: restart collectors after secret sync, verify middleware traffic stops, and alert the owning team if requests continue.

Case I — Nightly snapshot job failed silently so ttl_days appears stuck at nine hundred ninety-nine: repair the collector credential, backfill snapshot events, and temporarily lower scheduling interval until stability returns.

Case J — Middleware log volume spike from health checks pollutes rpm_med: exclude health paths at ingest with a transforms rule or raise thresholds for known probe service accounts labeled in inventory.

Case K — Authentication model join returns null auth_evt_cnt for legitimate service principals: confirm Authentication.user extraction maps to owner_login in the lookup or switch the enrichment arm to a secondary identity field documented in your field guide.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists four arms; required SPL verbs appear; final table includes sixteen analyst-visible columns including auth_evt_cnt for Authentication model enrichment context.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the passwords machines use to talk to Grafana dashboards. When one lasts too long, shows up in the wrong place, or belongs to someone who left, we raise a clear signal so teams rotate it before charts and automation quietly break.
hardware: Self-managed Grafana OSS or Grafana Enterprise on Linux VMs or Kubernetes worker nodes; Splunk indexers receiving forwarder or HTTP Event Collector traffic; optional hardened bastion or cron container for HTTPS snapshot exports.
